introSection: Skyrocket was a wooden roller coaster that operated at Boardwalk Park in Indiana and later at Lakeview Park in Michigan.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster opened as King Bee with Boardwalk Park in Whiting, Indiana on July 4, 1925. It operated for three years. It was dismantled with the park in 1929.

This roller coaster was relocated from Boardwalk Park with the assistance of John A. Miller. It reopened at Lakeview Park in Battle Creek, Michigan as Skyrocket on 23 June 1929.

The coaster closed with the park in 1932 and was demolished in 1934.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Newspaper article with an image of the coaster

fullText:
Skyrocket was a wooden roller coaster that operated at Boardwalk Park in Indiana and later at Lakeview Park in Michigan.

The coaster opened as King Bee with Boardwalk Park in Whiting, Indiana on July 4, 1925. It operated for three years. It was dismantled with the park in 1929.

This roller coaster was relocated from Boardwalk Park with the assistance of John A. Miller. It reopened at Lakeview Park in Battle Creek, Michigan as Skyrocket on 23 June 1929.

The coaster closed with the park in 1932 and was demolished in 1934.


 * Newspaper article with an image of the coaster
